goal: Use the tuned HD96 content model as a teacher so an expanded-vocabulary curriculum model can learn clean aligned chunks without forgetting the original chunk behavior.
reason: Teacher distillation preserved the original strict held-out score almost perfectly, but the model still did not learn the new aligned chunks well enough.
interpretation: Distillation solved most of the forgetting problem: the best distilled model tied baseline exact match on strict text-held-out chunks.
It did not solve the adaptation problem: performance on the new aligned chunks remained very low.
The current official content baseline should stay unchanged.

The next architecture step should separate old and new behavior more explicitly, for example with an adapter or dual-head model, instead of forcing one shared CTC head to satisfy both distributions immediately.
next_step: Prototype an adapter/dual-head content model or train a separate expanded-content recognizer while keeping the old tuned content model as the production/default path.
